items.summary: Fix the timeframe display bug in the Results screen: the 'Time Frame' tile in the 2×2 info grid must display the timeframe actually selected by the user in Settings (M1, M3, or M5), not always default to M1. The timeframe must be read from the analysis result's 'timeframe' field (which is populated by localCandleAnalysis.ts reading from localStorage/settings). Additionally, update the countdown timer initialization so M1=60s, M3=180s, M5=300s — matching the selected timeframe exactly. The 'Entrar às HH:MM' static label must compute entry time as current time at page load plus the correct duration for the selected timeframe.

Fix the timeframe propagation in frontend/src/services/localCandleAnalysis.ts: when reading the user's selected timeframe from localStorage/settings, ensure the value is read at analysis execution time (not at module load time). The key used to read from localStorage must exactly match the key used by the Settings page to persist the timeframe. If the stored value is 'M3', 'M5', or 'M1', store it verbatim in the LocalAnalysisResult's 'timeframe' field. Never fall back to 'M1' unless no stored value exists.

Update the Results screen layout to match the reference design shown in image-11.png and image-12.png. The layout must show: (1) Two side-by-side tiles at the top — left tile shows 'TEMPO' label with countdown timer in large bold white (e.g., '00:29') and 'restante' subtitle; right tile shows 'ENTRAR ÀS' label with the entry time in cyan/teal (e.g., '19:10') and 'horário de entrada' subtitle; (2) A large full-width green banner for COMPRA (with '↗ COMPRA' text) or red for VENDA, showing 'Tendência: ALTA/BAIXA', 'Confiança: XX%', and a 'Força: forte/média/fraca' badge inline within the banner; (3) A 2×2 grid of dark tiles showing Tendência (with colored arrow), Precisão (with value), Volume (with value), Time Frame (with selected timeframe value); (4) The chart thumbnail displayed below the grid as a scrollable secondary section with support/resistance overlays.

Fix the Settings page timeframe selector in frontend/src/pages/Settings.tsx to correctly persist the selected value (M1, M3, or M5) to localStorage and to the backend via the updateSettings mutation. Audit the localStorage key name used by Settings.tsx when saving the timeframe and ensure it is identical to the key read by localCandleAnalysis.ts. After saving, show the Portuguese success toast 'Configuração salva!'. Verify that selecting M3 or M5 and navigating away then returning to Settings still shows the previously saved value (not reverted to M1).

Ensure the analysis explanation text (explicacao field) generated by frontend/src/services/localCandleAnalysis.ts is unique per analysis and meaningfully references the specific detected patterns, trend direction, and candle characteristics found in the particular image being analyzed. Prevent generic or repetitive phrases from being reused across different chart analyses by incorporating the actual computed values (trend direction, pattern names, confidence score, momentum count) into the explanation template dynamically.
